name: EmbedUpload
summary: Candidate mirror-uploader service pending first-party verification for upload-once fan-out across multiple downstream hosts.

account.benefits: Account requirements and feature differences are still pending first-party review.

developer.notes: Developer and automation support have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types probably depend on downstream hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The user-provided seed link was HTTP-only, and current HTTPS behavior still needs verification.

max_file_size.notes: Detailed mirror-upload limits for this candidate have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

guest_uploads.notes: Account requirements and feature differences are still pending first-party review.

remote_import.notes: Current first-party sources for this candidate have not yet verified a remote URL import workflow.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party sources for this candidate have not yet verified a torrent import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Standalone storage behavior for this mirror-uploader candidate has not yet been verified.

retention_model.notes: Retention likely depends on downstream mirrored hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

downstream_dependency.notes: Bandwidth behavior has not yet been verified from first-party sources.

name: Mirrorace
summary: Mirror-uploader service for uploading a file once and fanning it out to multiple downstream file hosts.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are available. Accounts add file management, own-host credentials, API credentials, and other management features.

developer.notes: MirrorAce publishes official API documentation for upload, remote upload, file info, and account-authenticated requests.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types probably depend on downstream hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The seed entry uses an HTTPS homepage, but deeper security claims still need first-party verification.

sources.label: MirrorAce homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing MirrorAce as a file mirroring service with remote URL uploads and file management.

sources.label: MirrorAce FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering 5 GB upload size, guest uploads, non-hosting behavior, and retention of mirrored links.

sources.label: MirrorAce account types
sources.notes: Official account-types page showing 10 GB file size for no-account and free-account uploads.

sources.label: MirrorAce API documentation
sources.notes: Official API docs for upload, remote upload, file info, authenticated API credentials, and a current example response showing a 10 GB max_file_size.

max_file_size.notes: Current official account-types and API surfaces show 10 GB limits for active no-account and free-account uploads. The FAQ still mentions 5 GB, so older help text appears stale.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are available. Accounts add file management, own-host credentials, API credentials, and other management features.

remote_import.notes: MirrorAce publishes remote URL upload workflows on the homepage and in the API docs.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages reviewed here do not clearly publish a torrent import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: MirrorAce says it is not a file-hosting site and keeps mirror links rather than hosting the mirrored files itself.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: MirrorAce says links remain in its database unless they violate terms, while the underlying mirrored files ultimately depend on downstream hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored file availability and download behavior ultimately depend on the selected downstream hosts.

name: Mirrored
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads files to multiple downstream hosts and returns an aggregate download page.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

developer.notes: Mirrored publishes official HTTPS API documentation for uploads, account info, and file information.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types probably depend on downstream hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The seed entry uses an HTTPS homepage, but deeper security claims still need first-party verification.

sources.label: Mirrored homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing Mirrored as a multi-host mirror uploader and showing current free-vs-PRO per-file upload limits.

sources.label: Mirrored FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering anonymous uploads, non-hosting behavior, and mirror-service workflow.

sources.label: Mirrored API docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for authenticated HTTPS uploads and file/account info.

max_file_size.notes: The current official homepage says regular users can upload up to 750 MB per file, while PRO users can upload up to 3 GB per file.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

remote_import.notes: Current first-party pages reviewed here do not clearly publish a remote URL import workflow.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages reviewed here do not clearly publish a torrent import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Official FAQ says Mirrored is not a file-hosting site; mirrored files live on downstream hosts and Mirrored keeps aggregate links.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: Mirrored stores aggregate links and background-mirrors files to downstream hosts, so long-term availability depends on both Mirrored's link database and the selected mirror hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored availability and download behavior depend on the selected downstream hosts and their own rules.
